prose:
Calculation summary – HOMO–LUMO gap of water

Method-provenance block
• Property: frontier-orbital energies (HOMO, LUMO) and HOMO–LUMO gap (eV)
• Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP, no density fitting (exact four-center integrals)
• Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates in /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/water/water.xyz; single-point calculation (no re-optimization)
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation)
• Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet)
• Software/backend: PySCF (invoked via chemkit frontier-orbitals skill)
• Convergence & caveats: SCF converged in 7 cycles; no warnings reported; integrity.trustworthy = true

Headline result
HOMO energy  =  −8.6037183 eV
LUMO energy  =  +0.33695037 eV
HOMO–LUMO gap = 8.9406687 eV

Reproducibility
Command (from cli_invocation):
/ home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/water/water.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp1q0hrc8f/live.json

Live .out log was written during execution alongside the JSON result in the same scratch directory (path printed at run start and available for inspection). No warnings were reported by the engine.

Thus, the computed gas-phase B3LYP/def2-TZVP HOMO–LUMO gap of H₂O is 8.9406687 eV.
